(1 comment)

Hmm.  I believe it's possible to setattr without open(), but I can't recall what's required to do that.  It would be good to add that if possible, since I assume the setfattr is failing because of the open().  [Maybe ask in Skype, someone probably knows what command(s) do that.]

Patch Set 9:

(2 comments)

With strace, I cannot see any open on the file on which getfattr/setfattr is called, only a getxattr/setxattr syscall.
And for regular attributes, I did not find any other way to get or set them than doing an ioctl, which means opening the file first.

Hmm, it seems there are still issues on the node when selinux is enforced.

lfs command generates a "Floating point exception" error and we can see the following in dmesg:

   [  402.229291] traps: lfs[27681] trap divide error ip:40e4da sp:7ffed7efd1b0 error:0 in lfs[400000+23000]

parents.subject: LU-12133 tests: sanityn test_35 syntax error

commit.subject: LU-8955 tests: exercise SELinux policy info
commit.message:
LU-8955 tests: exercise SELinux policy info

Add new tests 21a and 21b to sanity-selinux.sh. Goal is to test
that SELinux policy info is properly sent by the client, and
checked by the server, in the following cases:
- connection
- create
- open
- unlink
- rename
- getxattr
- setxattr
- setattr
- getattr
- symlink
- hardlink

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-selinux clientselinux
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: Ibd9c5added027e12d8126641c56f21fdbc791941
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/33699
Tested-by: Jenkins
